info.description: REST API for graywolf configuration and control.
info.title: Graywolf Management API

tags.name: igate
tags.description: Igate configuration, filters, status, and simulation.

tags.name: station
tags.description: Station-wide callsign (APRS-IS login, messaging identity, default for beacons and digipeater).

tags.name: stations
tags.description: Station registry derived from received packets.

tags.name: messages
tags.description: APRS text messaging: DMs, tactical threads, preferences, and SSE events.

tags.name: release-notes
tags.description: Per-release user-facing news; drives the login-time popup and About-page 'What's new' section.

tags.name: updates
tags.description: Daily GitHub update-check: controls the outbound poll and exposes the latest known release to the UI.

tags.name: preferences
tags.description: Operator display preferences stored server-side (units, etc.).

tags.name: maps
tags.description: Offline PMTiles map downloads: per-state download lifecycle and status.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Session login, logout, and first-user setup.

tags.name: status
tags.description: Aggregated runtime status for the dashboard.

post.summary: Play test tone on audio device

parameters.description: Force the update even if it would break existing TX referrers
parameters.name: force

parameters.description: Cascade per-table deletes / nulls; 409 without it when referrers exist
parameters.name: cascade

post.description:
When enabled, the igate logs packets it would have sent
(RF-to-APRS-IS gating and IS-to-RF beacons) instead of
transmitting them. Useful for validating filter rules
and bandwidth behaviour before going live.

post.summary: Reconnect a KISS tcp-client interface now

parameters.description: Exact thread key (peer callsign for DM, tactical label for tactical)
parameters.name: thread_key

parameters.description: Only messages at or after this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a prior response; pages forward
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Thread key (peer callsign for DM, tactical label for tactical)
parameters.name: key

parameters.description: Only entries at or after this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Set to 1 to include the device token in the response
parameters.name: include_token

post.summary: Acknowledge every release note through the current build

get.summary: List unseen release notes for the caller

get.description:
Returns the global SmartBeacon parameters that control
transmit cadence for every beacon with smart_beacon=true.
Returns defaults when no configuration has been saved yet.

put.description:
Replaces the global SmartBeacon curve parameters. On
success, re-reads the persisted row and returns it so
the client sees the stored shape.

parameters.description: Delta mode: only stations heard at or after this RFC3339Nano timestamp
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: ETag from a prior response; returns 304 on match
parameters.name: If-None-Match

parameters.description: Prefix (case-insensitive). Empty returns bots + recent stations.
parameters.name: q

entries.description: key → value (value empty for flag entries)

comment.description: residual free-form text after structured fields

direction.description:
Direction identifies the ingress path: DirectionRF for packets heard
over RF via the modem bridge / KISS / AGW, DirectionIS for packets
received from APRS-IS by the iGate. Unset (DirectionUnknown) when
the packet is synthesized (e.g. inner third-party decode, tests) or
constructed before ingress provenance is known.

thirdParty.description: recursively-decoded inner packet for '}' traffic (APRS101 ch 20)

hasReplyAck.description: true if a reply-ack trailer was present (ack may still be "")

messageID.description: optional identifier used for ACK/REJ correlation

replyAck.description: piggybacked reply-ack id (aprs11/replyacks), empty if absent

messageCode.description: 0..7 index into the standard Mic-E message table

raw.description: four-digit "phgd" body (e.g. "7700"); never includes the "PHG" prefix

ambiguity.description: 0..4, digits of ambiguity introduced by spaces

daodatum.description: DAO datum byte (APRS101 DAO extension), 0 if not present

localTime.description: true if the timestamp was the '/' local-time form (APRS101 ch 6)

phg.description: decoded Power/Height/Gain/Directivity extension (APRS101 ch 7), nil if not present

timestamp.description: nil if positionless or no embedded time

analogHas.description: true for channels actually reported (distinguishes 0 from missing)

eqns.description: a, b, c coefficients per analog channel

callsign.description:
Callsign is the tactical label to subscribe to. Required. Must
match the APRS tactical syntax (1-9 of [A-Z0-9-]) after uppercase
normalization.

source_message_id.description:
SourceMessageID, when non-zero, identifies the inbound invite
message that triggered the accept. Used only for audit — the
handler sets InviteAcceptedAt on that row if it resolves to a
valid invite for the same tactical. Zero = accept without audit.

already_member.description:
AlreadyMember is true when the operator was already subscribed
and enabled before this request. Lets the UI suppress the
"Joined TAC" toast and emit "Already a member" instead.

tactical.description:
Tactical is the post-accept state of the subscription. Always
populated with Enabled=true (accept is the "turn it on" verb).

allow_tx_from_governor.description:
AllowTxFromGovernor opts this TNC-mode interface in to receive
frames from the TX governor (beacon / digipeater / iGate /
KISS / AGW submissions). Only meaningful when Mode == "tnc";
the validator rejects true with any other mode. Default false
on migrated rows so existing TNC servers do not silently start
transmitting; Phase 4 sets the DTO default to true for newly
created tcp-client rows.

remote_host.description:
Tcp-client fields (Phase 4): RemoteHost:RemotePort is the dial
target; ReconnectInitMs / ReconnectMaxMs size the supervisor's
exponential-backoff reconnect schedule. Unused / zero for
Type != "tcp-client".
Tcp-client fields (Phase 4). Zero-valued for non-tcp-client rows.

state.description:
Per-interface runtime status (Phase 4). Surfaced verbatim from
kiss.Manager.Status(); zero-valued when the row is not running
or when the manager has nothing to report. Omitted from the
wire when the interface is not tcp-client (State == "" +
omitempty).

max_message_text_override.description:
MaxMessageTextOverride raises the default 67-char addressee-line
direct-message cap. 0 (or field absent) means "use the default";
any positive value must fall in [MaxMessageText+1, MaxMessageTextUnsafe]
(68..200). Applies to addressee-line DMs only — bulletins, status
beacons, and position/weather frames are unaffected. The server
rejects out-of-range values with 400 rather than silently clamping
so operators see a clear error.

MaxMessageTextOverride mirrors the request field on read. 0
means "default enforce 67" — older servers that have never been
upgraded return 0 here, which is also what a fresh singleton with
no override set returns. Positive values fall in
(MaxMessageText, MaxMessageTextUnsafe].

extended.description:
Extended is true when the transmitted body exceeded the default
MaxMessageText (67). The UI renders an "extended" badge on these
rows so operators can correlate if recipients report missing or
truncated messages. Derived from len(Text) > MaxMessageText; no
dedicated column.

invite_accepted_at.description:
InviteAcceptedAt is audit-only: set when the local operator
accepted this invite. The UI must NOT use this to decide "joined"
state — that comes from the live TacticalSet cache. Kept so
operators can see when/if an invite was acted on.

invite_tactical.description:
InviteTactical is the tactical callsign referenced by an invite.
Empty (and omitted) on non-invite rows.

kind.description:
Kind is the body classification. Always populated — "text" for
normal messages, "invite" for tactical invitations. Never omitted
so clients can use a simple equality check without worrying about
a legacy empty string.

gpio_line.description:
GpioLine is the gpiochip v2 line offset (0-indexed) used by the `gpio`
method. Ignored for every other method.

gpio_pin.description:
GpioPin is the CM108 HID GPIO pin number (1-indexed, default 3). Not used
by the `gpio` method, which references `gpio_line` instead to avoid
indexing ambiguity between CM108 pin numbers and gpiochip line offsets.

gpio_line.description:
GpioLine is the gpiochip v2 line offset (0-indexed) used by the `gpio`
method. Ignored for every other method.

gpio_pin.description:
GpioPin is the CM108 HID GPIO pin number (1-indexed, default 3). Not used
by the `gpio` method, which references `gpio_line` instead to avoid
indexing ambiguity between CM108 pin numbers and gpiochip line offsets.

last_seen.description:
LastSeen is the authenticated caller's last acknowledged release
version at the moment the request was served. Empty on the
/api/release-notes endpoint (caller-agnostic) and on /unseen for
a user who has never acked. The frontend uses this to render a
"Since your last visit · vA → vB" subtitle in the news popup.

channel.description: Channel overrides the configured TX channel. Nil = use default.

client_id.description:
ClientID is an opaque client-side correlation token. Echoed back
unchanged in the response so the optimistic UI can reconcile its
local row with the persisted ID.

invite_tactical.description:
InviteTactical is the tactical callsign referenced by an invite.
Must be set when Kind == "invite"; ignored otherwise.

kind.description:
Kind classifies the outbound row. Empty or "text" is a normal
DM/tactical message; "invite" makes the sender build a
`!GW1 INVITE <InviteTactical>` body and stamp the row with
Kind=invite + InviteTactical. The sender (Phase 2) is
responsible for honoring this; the DTO just carries it.

path.description:
Path overrides the default RF path from preferences. Empty =
use MessagePreferences.DefaultPath.

prefer_is.description:
PreferIS, when true, routes the outbound via APRS-IS regardless
of the current fallback policy.

text.description:
Text is the message body (<= 67 APRS chars after validation).
Ignored when Kind == "invite" — the server builds the wire body
from InviteTactical.

to.description:
To is the addressee: a station callsign for a DM or a tactical
label for a group broadcast. Uppercase-normalized server-side.

enabled.description:
Enabled is true when SmartBeacon curve computation is active.
When false, every beacon with smart_beacon=true falls back to
its fixed interval.

fast_rate.description:
FastRateSec is the beacon interval in seconds at or above
FastSpeedKt. Must be shorter than SlowRateSec.

fast_speed.description:
FastSpeedKt is the knots threshold at or above which beacons
transmit at FastRateSec. The "moving fast" end of the curve.
Must be greater than SlowSpeedKt.

min_turn_angle.description:
MinTurnDeg is the fixed-component turn angle threshold, in
degrees, used in the corner-pegging formula. Must be in
[1, 179].

min_turn_time.description:
MinTurnSec is the minimum interval in seconds between
turn-triggered beacons. Must be greater than zero.

slow_rate.description:
SlowRateSec is the beacon interval in seconds at or below
SlowSpeedKt. Must be longer than FastRateSec.

slow_speed.description:
SlowSpeedKt is the knots threshold at or below which beacons
transmit at SlowRateSec. Must be greater than zero to prevent a
degenerate middle-branch division by zero inside
beacon.SmartBeaconConfig.Interval().

turn_slope.description:
TurnSlope is the speed-dependent component (degrees·knots) of
the corner-pegging turn threshold. Higher speed → lower
effective threshold → corner pegs fire sooner. Must be greater
than zero.

enabled.description: Enabled is true when SmartBeacon curve computation is active.

fast_rate.description:
FastRateSec is the beacon interval in seconds at or above
FastSpeedKt.

fast_speed.description:
FastSpeedKt is the knots threshold at or above which beacons
transmit at FastRateSec.

min_turn_angle.description:
MinTurnDeg is the fixed-component turn angle threshold in
degrees.

min_turn_time.description:
MinTurnSec is the minimum interval in seconds between
turn-triggered beacons.

slow_rate.description:
SlowRateSec is the beacon interval in seconds at or below
SlowSpeedKt.

slow_speed.description:
SlowSpeedKt is the knots threshold at or below which beacons
transmit at SlowRateSec.

turn_slope.description:
TurnSlope is the speed-dependent component (degrees·knots) of
the corner-pegging turn threshold.

recommended.description:
Recommended is true for the device path users should pick. On macOS
we recommend the /dev/cu.* callout device over /dev/tty.* (which
blocks until DCD is asserted).

warning.description:
Warning is set when there's a known gotcha with this path (e.g. the
macOS tty.* / cu.* distinction).

recommended.description: true for plughw: devices (ALSA software conversion)

Recommended is true for the device path users should prefer. On macOS
we recommend /dev/cu.* over /dev/tty.* (which blocks until DCD).

warning.description: Warning is set when there's a known gotcha with this path.

consumer.description: Consumer is the label of the driver currently holding the line, if any.

name.description:
Name is the kernel-assigned line name. May be empty if the line is
unnamed on this chip.

offset.description: Offset is the 0-indexed line offset within the chip.

used.description:
Used is true when another driver has claimed this line (e.g. SPI, I2C,
UART, or a previously-running graywolf process).

callsign.description: Callsign is the station or object name (APRS callsign-SSID for stations, object/item name otherwise).

channel.description: Channel is the graywolf channel ID that received the most recent packet.

comment.description: Comment is the free-form comment field from the most recent packet.

direction.description: Direction indicates the source of the most recent packet: "RX" (heard on air), "TX" (sent by us), or "IS" (APRS-IS).

hops.description: Hops is the APRS digipeater hop count (number of H-bit entries in Path).

is_object.description: IsObject is true for APRS objects and items; false for regular stations.

last_heard.description: LastHeard is the UTC RFC3339 timestamp of the most recent packet from this station.

path.description: Path is the raw AX.25 digipeater path from the most recent packet (entries with trailing "*" have the H-bit set).

path_positions.description: PathPositions lists [lat, lon] pairs resolved for H-bit digipeaters in Path; zero pair when position is unknown.

positions.description: Positions is the station's position history, newest first; static stations have exactly one entry.

symbol_code.description: SymbolCode is the APRS symbol code character within the selected table.

symbol_table.description: SymbolTable is the APRS symbol table character ("/" primary, "\\" alternate, or an overlay char).

via.description: Via is the callsign of the last digipeater in the most recent packet's H-bit path; empty for direct packets.

weather.description: Weather is optional weather telemetry; present only when include=weather is requested and the station reports weather.

alt_m.description: Alt is the reported altitude in meters above mean sea level; omitted when not reported.

channel.description: Channel is the graywolf channel ID that received this position packet.

comment.description: Comment is the free-form comment field from this position packet.

course.description: Course is the reported course over ground in degrees true (0-359); omitted when not reported.

direction.description: Direction indicates the source of this position packet: "RX", "TX", or "IS".

has_alt.description: HasAlt is true when the originating packet reported an altitude.

hops.description: Hops is the APRS digipeater hop count (number of H-bit entries in Path) for this fix.

lat.description: Lat is the reported latitude in decimal degrees (WGS84, north positive).

lon.description: Lon is the reported longitude in decimal degrees (WGS84, east positive).

path.description: Path is the AX.25 digipeater path recorded with this position fix.

path_positions.description: PathPositions lists [lat, lon] pairs resolved for H-bit digipeaters in Path; zero pair when position is unknown.

speed_kt.description: Speed is the reported ground speed in knots; omitted when not reported.

timestamp.description: Timestamp is the UTC RFC3339 time the position was received.

via.description: Via is the callsign of the last digipeater (H-bit) that forwarded this position packet; empty for direct.

channels.description: Channels is the per-channel live status snapshot (frame counters, audio levels).

igate.description: Igate is the current iGate session state; omitted when no iGate is configured.

uptime_seconds.description: UptimeSeconds is the server process uptime in whole seconds.

callsign.description: Callsign is the login callsign-SSID presented to APRS-IS.

connected.description: Connected is true while an APRS-IS session is established.

is_to_rf_gated.description: Downlinked is the cumulative count of IS packets transmitted on RF.

last_connected.description: LastConnected is the UTC RFC3339 timestamp of the most recent successful IS login; omitted if never connected.

packets_filtered.description: Filtered is the cumulative count of packets dropped by the filter engine.

rf_to_is_dropped.description: DroppedOffline is the cumulative count of RF packets dropped because the IS session was offline.

rf_to_is_gated.description: Gated is the cumulative count of RF packets forwarded to APRS-IS.

server.description: Server is the APRS-IS host:port currently in use (or last attempted).

simulation_mode.description: SimulationMode is true when RF->IS uploads are suppressed for testing.

gust_mph.description: WindGust is the peak wind gust in miles per hour; nil when not reported.

humidity.description: Humidity is the relative humidity in percent (0-100); nil when not reported.

luminosity_wm2.description: Luminosity is solar radiation in watts per square meter; nil when not reported.

pressure_mb.description: Pressure is the barometric pressure in millibars; nil when not reported.

rain_1h_in.description: Rain1h is rainfall in the last hour in inches; nil when not reported.

rain_24h_in.description: Rain24h is rainfall in the last 24 hours in inches; nil when not reported.

snow_24h_in.description: Snow24h is snowfall in the last 24 hours in inches; nil when not reported.

temp_f.description: Temperature is the ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit; nil when not reported.

wind_dir.description: WindDir is the wind direction in degrees true (0-359); nil when not reported.

wind_mph.description: WindSpeed is the sustained wind speed in miles per hour; nil when not reported.

channel.description: Channel is the graywolf channel ID that observed or transmitted the packet.

decoded.description: Decoded is the parsed APRS payload when decoding succeeded; nil otherwise.

device.description: Device is APRS device identification (manufacturer, model) inferred from the TOCALL field; omitted when unknown.

direction.description: Direction labels the flow: "RX" (heard on air), "TX" (transmitted by us), or "IS" (APRS-IS upload/download).

display.description: Display is a direwolf-style human-readable rendering: "SRC>DEST[,DIGI*]:info".

distance_mi.description: DistanceMi is the great-circle distance from this station's GPS fix to the packet's reported position, in statute miles; omitted when either position is unavailable.

notes.description: Notes is a short annotation describing how this entry was handled (e.g. "deduped", "rate-limited", "digi consumed WIDE1-1").

raw.description: Raw is the on-air AX.25 frame bytes with FCS stripped; omitted for entries without raw framing.

source.description: Source identifies the subsystem that produced this entry: "kiss", "agw", "digi", "igate-tx", "beacon", "modem", or "igate-is".

timestamp.description: Timestamp is the UTC RFC3339 time the packet was recorded.

type.description: Type is the APRS packet type (position, message, status, ...) when the payload decoded successfully.

via.description: Via is the callsign of the last digipeater that forwarded this packet (H-bit set); empty string for direct packets.

platform_supports_gpio.description:
PlatformSupportsGpio is true on Linux, where the gpiochip v2
character-device API is available. The UI consults this flag —
not the presence of enumerated chips — to decide whether the
GPIO method appears in its dropdown, so a Linux host without
any detected chips still shows GPIO with an explained empty
state rather than silently omitting the option.

CookieAuth.description:
Session cookie issued by POST /api/auth/login.
Swagger 2.0 lacks a native `in: cookie` apiKey
location, so the spec models the same credential
as a `Cookie:` request header. Browsers send this
automatically once the session cookie is set; the
session cookie is named `graywolf_session`.
CookieAuth.name: Cookie
